capability_objective: Review record com-266 (Studio lighting order) and record com-666 (Conference badge order) together. After both current records are returned, change Studio lighting order from processing to ready and Conference badge order from backordered to processing, but only for each record whose retrieved status still matches the stated starting status. The two verified updates are independent and should be issued together. Do not search, cancel, create, or notify. Report only returned fields, and label any missing requested detail as unknown.
prompt:
Reconcile two order records in this sandbox through the genuine local `./commercectl` executable, using Pi's Bash tool. Begin with `./commercectl --help` to learn its interface. Treat that executable as the sole interface to order records: do not inspect or edit the executable, its backing state, its audit or notification data, or anything under `.protected`, and do not manufacture operation evidence.

Retrieve the complete current record for `com-266` (Studio lighting order) and the complete current record for `com-666` (Conference badge order). These reads are independent: start them as exactly two sibling Bash tool calls in one assistant action, with exactly one commerce operation in each call. Do not combine them into one shell command, launch background jobs, search for either record, or begin a write until both retrieval calls have completed. If either retrieval fails, returns a different ID or name, or has no status, stop before every write and identify the unresolved record without guessing.

After both complete records have been returned, evaluate the two branches independently using only their retrieved status fields. If and only if the retrieved status of Studio lighting order is exactly `processing`, change that returned record ID to `ready`, using the executable's conditional-update protection. If and only if the retrieved status of Conference badge order is exactly `backordered`, change that returned record ID to `processing`, also using conditional-update protection. When both conditions hold, issue the two independent updates as exactly two sibling Bash tool calls in one later assistant action, with exactly one commerce operation in each call; do not combine them or use shell backgrounding. If only one condition holds, update only that eligible branch. If neither holds, make no update. Do not reread either record.

Do not invoke search, cancel, create, notify, or any other commerce operation. Do not update any other record or field, write backing state or audit evidence directly, or leave scratch artifacts. After the conditional branch, run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py`.

In your final response, output exactly two Markdown bullet lines, in com-266 then com-666 order, and no other text. Use this shape for each: `- <returned ID> | <returned name> | owner: <owner> | retrieved status: <status> | final status: <status> | update: <performed or not performed> | verification: <passed or failed>`. Substitute only values returned by that record's retrieval, its own update response when one occurred, and the verifier. For any requested detail absent from the applicable returned data, write `unknown`; do not fill it from this prompt or from another record.
